main.question: Do I need a boat licence to rent a boat in Rhodes?
main.answer: Not necessarily, and this surprises many first-time visitors to Rhodes. Greek maritime law allows anyone aged 18 or over to operate a pleasure craft up to 30 horsepower without holding a formal boating licence, provided they carry valid identification.

main.question: What is included in the price of a boat tour or cruise?
main.answer: That depends on the type of experience you book, and we always send a written breakdown before you confirm so there are no surprises at the dock.

main.question: How far in advance should I book?
main.answer: Timing depends on the season, the type of experience, and how flexible your dates are. During peak season from mid-June through August, we recommend booking at least one to two weeks in advance, especially for private yacht charters, weekend speedboat tours, and popular shared Symi day cruises that fill quickly.

main.question: What happens if the weather is bad on the day of my trip?
main.answer: Safety always comes first, no booking is worth putting guests or crew at risk in unsuitable sea conditions. Our skippers and rental operators monitor weather forecasts daily, including wind speed, direction, and swell along the east coast and on open crossings toward Symi or Halki.

main.question: Can I bring children on board?
main.answer: Absolutely, many of our most memorable days at sea involve families with children from toddlers to teenagers. We carry child-size life jackets on all boats and require every guest to wear one while underway according to Greek maritime regulations.

main.question: What is the difference between a boat tour, a cruise, and a charter?
main.answer: These terms are often used interchangeably online, but they describe very different experiences on the water around Rhodes. A boat tour is typically a guided day trip on a smaller vessel with a professional skipper, either private (your group only) or semi-private (up to about 12 guests). The route focuses on swimming, snorkelling and sightseeing along the coast, usually lasting three to eight hours.

main.question: Do your skippers speak English?
main.answer: Yes, all of our skippers and crew members speak fluent English, and communication is never a problem on any experience we arrange. Rhodes has been a major tourism destination for decades, and professional boat operators invest in crew who can explain safety procedures, discuss routes, and share local knowledge clearly with international guests.

main.question: What should I bring on a boat trip?
main.answer: Packing light and sensibly makes the day more comfortable for everyone onboard. We recommend high-SPF sunscreen applied before boarding and reapplied after swimming, a wide-brim hat, UV-protective sunglasses, and a quick-dry towel.

main.question: Can I book a private experience for a special occasion?
main.answer: Of course, some of our most rewarding bookings are celebrations where the sea provides the setting. We regularly arrange private charters and skippered boat trips for birthdays, proposals, anniversaries, honeymoons, and milestone family gatherings, as well as corporate events and team-building days for companies visiting Rhodes.

main.question: How do I get in touch with you?
main.answer: You can reach us by phone at +30 695 166 6454, by email at info@rhodesrentaboat.com, by WhatsApp, or through the contact form on our website. We respond to every message personally, usually within a few hours during the season, and always within one business day.

headTerms.question: How much does a boat trip in Rhodes cost?
headTerms.answer: A boat trip in Rhodes typically costs €40–€80 per person on shared day cruises that include lunch, soft drinks and several swim stops along the east coast. Private boat trips are priced per vessel: half-day skippered trips often start around €350–€500, full-day private trips €600–€900 for groups of four to eight.

headTerms.question: What is the best boat trip from Rhodes?
headTerms.answer: The best boat trip from Rhodes depends on what you want from the day, not a single brochure winner. The Symi day cruise is the most booked route: boats leave Mandraki around 09:00, you spend four to five hours in the pastel harbour of Gialos, and return to Rhodes by about 18:00—a full island day without overnight luggage.

headTerms.question: Can you visit Symi in one day from Rhodes?
headTerms.answer: Yes—you can visit Symi in one day from Rhodes without staying overnight. Shared day cruises and excursion boats leave Mandraki Harbour around 09:00, cross in roughly 75–90 minutes when the sea is calm, and give you four to five hours ashore in Gialos harbour before the return sailing, typically arriving back in Rhodes by 18:00.

home.question: How much does a boat trip in Rhodes cost?
home.answer: A boat trip in Rhodes typically costs €40–€80 per person on shared day cruises that include lunch, soft drinks and several swim stops along the east coast. Private boat trips are priced per vessel: half-day skippered trips often start around €350–€500, full-day private trips €600–€900 for groups of four to eight.

home.question: What is the best boat trip from Rhodes?
home.answer: The best boat trip from Rhodes depends on what you want from the day, not a single brochure winner. The Symi day cruise is the most booked route: boats leave Mandraki around 09:00, you spend four to five hours in the pastel harbour of Gialos, and return to Rhodes by about 18:00—a full island day without overnight luggage.

home.question: Can you visit Symi in one day from Rhodes?
home.answer: Yes—you can visit Symi in one day from Rhodes without staying overnight. Shared day cruises and excursion boats leave Mandraki Harbour around 09:00, cross in roughly 75–90 minutes when the sea is calm, and give you four to five hours ashore in Gialos harbour before the return sailing, typically arriving back in Rhodes by 18:00.

charter.question: Do I need a licence to charter a yacht in Rhodes?
charter.answer: For a bareboat charter, where you skipper the yacht yourself, you need a recognised sailing qualification such as RYA Day Skipper or higher, ASA, IYT or an ICC accepted under Greek maritime rules. Greek law also requires a co-skipper with at least basic sailing experience on board for bareboat rentals.

charter.question: Bareboat or skippered, which should I pick?
charter.answer: Bareboat suits experienced sailors who want to plan their own route, anchor where they like and run the boat themselves without a captain on board. You need valid qualifications, confidence in Mediterranean conditions including the Meltemi, and willingness to handle provisioning, marina fees and night passages.

charter.question: How much does a yacht charter in Rhodes cost?
charter.answer: Weekly bareboat rates typically run €1,500–€7,500 for monohull sailing yachts from about 35 to 55 feet, and €3,500–€15,000 or more for catamarans, depending on size, age and season. Skipper service is around €180–€250 per day on top of the yacht fee. Peak weeks from mid-July to late August command the highest prices; June and September are often twenty to thirty percent cheaper for the same boat.

charter.question: When is the best time for a sailing holiday in the Dodecanese?
charter.answer: May, June, September and early October are the sweet spot for a Dodecanese sailing holiday based out of Rhodes, warm sea, lighter winds and emptier anchorages than in high summer. July and August bring the strong Meltemi north winds, often Force four to seven, which experienced sailors enjoy but families with young children may find tiring on longer legs.

charter.question: Do charters always start on a Saturday?
charter.answer: Yes, Greek bareboat charters traditionally start on Saturday and run for seven nights, with embarkation in the late afternoon and disembarkation by 09:00 the following Saturday. In practice a “weekly” charter gives you about six full days of sailing because the first and last days involve handover, inventory checks and returning to base.

charter.question: Where can I sail to from Rhodes?
charter.answer: Rhodes is an excellent base for exploring the Dodecanese. Typical seven-day routes loop Symi, Tilos, Halki, Nisyros and back to Rhodes; longer itineraries reach Kos, Leros and Patmos for guests with more time and fair weather. The east coast of Rhodes alone offers Lindos, Kalithea, Faliraki bays and quiet anchorages for shorter loops or first-time charterers who prefer gentle days.

rentABoat.question: Do I really need no licence to rent a boat in Rhodes?
rentABoat.answer: Yes, Greek law allows visitors to operate small motorboats up to 30 horsepower without a formal boating licence, provided you are over 18 and carry valid ID or passport. The license-free boats we offer fall well within that limit, typically four- to five-metre fibreglass boats with outboard engines designed for sheltered coastal cruising.

rentABoat.question: No licence vs with skipper, how do I decide?
rentABoat.answer: Pick a license-free self-drive boat if you want independence, you are comfortable handling a small motorboat after the briefing, and you plan to swim in your own bay at your own pace along the sheltered east coast.

rentABoat.question: How far can I go with a license-free boat?
rentABoat.answer: License-free boats are intended for coastal cruising within about one nautical mile from shore in good weather, along the east coast between Mandraki and bays such as Anthony Quinn, Ladiko and Kalithea Springs. That range covers plenty of swim spots, beach tavernas and photo opportunities without committing to an open-sea crossing.

rentABoat.question: What is included in the rental?
rentABoat.answer: A standard license-free rental includes the boat, fuel for the agreed coastal range, safety equipment such as life jackets for all sizes, first-aid kit and anchor, plus a marine map of the local area and a fifteen-minute safety briefing before you leave the dock. A cooler with ice is usually included so you can bring drinks and snacks.

rentABoat.question: What if the weather turns bad?
rentABoat.answer: Safety always comes first for self-drive rentals. If wind or sea conditions exceed safe limits for license-free boats on your booked day, we either move the rental to another suitable day during your stay or refund in full, we make the call early in the morning based on the official forecast and local knowledge, usually before you travel to the harbour.

rentABoat.question: Can I take children on board?
rentABoat.answer: Yes, children of any age are welcome on rental boats with properly fitted life jackets; we provide child-size jackets free of charge and require everyone to wear them while underway according to Greek rules. For families with very young children or toddlers, we usually recommend a boat with skipper so both parents can focus on the kids rather than navigation and anchoring.

rentABoat.question: How do I book and what are the payment terms?
rentABoat.answer: Send us a message, email or WhatsApp with your dates, group size and whether you prefer license-free or skippered, we confirm availability the same day during the season and send a written quote with everything included. A deposit secures the booking; the balance is typically paid on the day of the rental in cash or by card at the operator base, as confirmed in your voucher.

boatTours.question: What is a boat tour in Rhodes?
boatTours.answer: A boat tour in Rhodes is a guided experience on the water with a professional skipper who knows the coast, the weather patterns and the best swim stops for each season. The boat follows a planned route, or adjusts it when you book privately, stops in bays for swimming and snorkelling, and returns to harbour by a set time.

boatTours.question: How long does a boat tour usually last?
boatTours.answer: Half-day boat tours usually run three to four hours with one or two swim stops, ideal if you have evening plans or young children who tire easily. Full-day tours run six to eight hours with three or four stops, lunch on board or at a beach taverna, and enough time to snorkel and relax at each bay without rushing.

boatTours.question: What is included in a guided boat tour?
boatTours.answer: Most guided boat tours include the boat, fuel for the planned route, a professional skipper, safety gear such as life jackets, first-aid kit and anchor, plus snorkelling masks and basic refreshments, soft drinks and snacks on smaller tours, often a full buffet lunch and unlimited soft drinks plus wine and beer on larger all-inclusive cruises.

boatTours.question: Where do boat tours go?
boatTours.answer: The east coast of Rhodes is the main playground for daily boat tours, Anthony Quinn Bay, Kalithea Springs, Ladiko, Traganou Caves, Tsambika beach and the wider Lindos area with views of the Acropolis from the sea. Routes vary by tour length: half-day trips might focus on two nearby bays; full-day tours string together three or four stops with lunch between.

boatTours.question: Are boat tours private or shared?
boatTours.answer: We offer both formats because they serve different needs and budgets. Shared tours run on fixed schedules with other guests aboard, often up to twelve on smaller guided boats or more on large day cruises, and offer the best value per person. Private tours are your group only, with flexible route and timing, ideal for couples, families celebrating an occasion or anyone who wants the boat to themselves.

boatTours.question: Which organised boat tour is best for first-time visitors in Rhodes?
boatTours.answer: For most first-time visitors, a shared east-coast day cruise or a semi-private guided tour with lunch and several swim stops is the easiest introduction, you see Anthony Quinn Bay, Kalithea Springs and the coastline without planning routes or anchoring yourself. If you prefer a quieter deck and flexible timing, a private skippered tour on a smaller boat suits couples and families who want to set the pace.

boatTrips.question: How much does a boat trip in Rhodes cost?
boatTrips.answer: A boat trip in Rhodes typically costs €40–€80 per person on shared day cruises that include lunch, soft drinks and several swim stops along the east coast. Private boat trips are priced per vessel: half-day skippered trips often start around €350–€500, full-day private trips €600–€900 for groups of four to eight.

boatTrips.question: How much does a private boat trip in Rhodes cost?
boatTrips.answer: Private boat trips are priced per boat, not per person, which changes the maths for groups. Half-day private trips on a small motorboat with skipper often start around €350–€500 for four to six guests. Full-day private trips commonly run €600–€900 depending on route and vessel. Private catamaran day charters typically fall in the €920–€1,400 range for a half or full day with crew.

boatTrips.question: Is a private boat trip worth it compared to a shared tour?
boatTrips.answer: For groups of four or more, a private trip is often worth it financially and experientially, per-person cost approaches a shared cruise while you gain a flexible route, your own pace, no strangers on deck and the ability to stay longer in a bay you love. For couples, private is more a celebration or honeymoon choice than a strict budget decision: you pay for intimacy and control.

boatTrips.question: How is a private boat trip different from a guided tour or cruise?
boatTrips.answer: A private trip is your group alone on a small or mid-size boat with a dedicated skipper and a route you shape together within weather limits. A guided tour may still be private, but the term often covers semi-private setups with other guests and a more fixed programme.

boatTrips.question: How many people fit on a private boat?
boatTrips.answer: Capacity depends on vessel type and legal certification, not just brochure photos. Small motorboats and speedboats comfortably carry four to eight guests for day trips. Mid-size sailing or motor yachts handle eight to twelve with seated shade and swim access.

boatTrips.question: Can we choose the route on a private trip?
boatTrips.answer: Yes, flexible routing is the main reason guests book private. Tell the skipper what matters most, snorkelling at Anthony Quinn Bay, lunch at a beach taverna, sunset light along the Old Town walls, a longer reach toward Symi if conditions allow, and they design the day around it within safety and fuel limits agreed at booking.

boatTrips.question: How far in advance should I book a private boat trip?
boatTrips.answer: In peak season from mid-June through August, book one to two weeks ahead for most private boats, especially weekends and popular catamarans. Luxury yachts and repeat-request skippers may need two to three weeks notice in July and August because agents and returning guests reserve early. In May, late September and October, a few days notice is often enough unless you need a specific yacht model.

catamaran.question: Why choose a catamaran over a monohull boat?
catamaran.answer: Catamarans have two hulls instead of one, so they sit flat on the water with far less roll in light chop, the most noticeable difference for guests who worry about motion or want to walk around freely without bracing against tilt. You get more deck space, more shaded seating, stairs straight into the water from the stern, and stable trampolines at the bow for sunbathing.

catamaran.question: What is included on a catamaran day cruise?
catamaran.answer: A typical six-hour all-inclusive catamaran day cruise includes buffet lunch, Mediterranean dishes such as grilled chicken, Greek salad, pasta, hummus and bread, plus unlimited soft drinks and often wine and beer, snorkel masks, three or four swim stops in calm bays, and a music system for atmosphere. Towels and sun-deck cushions are commonly provided; some cruises add paddleboards or kayaks.

catamaran.question: Are catamaran tours good if I get sea-sick?
catamaran.answer: Yes, catamarans are the most stable mainstream option for day trips short of a large ferry, especially on coastal routes where waves are small. Twin hulls reduce rolling compared to monohull day boats, which matters for susceptible guests on three- to six-hour trips.

catamaran.question: Day cruise or sunset catamaran cruise, which to pick?
catamaran.answer: Day cruises of six to seven hours focus on swimming, snorkelling, lunch and multiple bays, best for first-time visitors, active groups and families who want a full day on the water. Sunset cruises of about three hours focus on evening light, dinner on board, drinks and the return view of Rhodes medieval walls, best for couples, anniversaries and a romantic mood without committing a whole day.

catamaran.question: Are catamaran tours private or shared?
catamaran.answer: Both exist in Rhodes and serve different budgets. Shared catamaran cruises run on fixed schedules with up to roughly thirty guests and deliver the best value per person with all-inclusive food and drinks, ideal when you want a social day without hiring the whole boat.

catamaran.question: How many people fit on a catamaran cruise?
catamaran.answer: Shared day-cruise catamarans are certified for roughly twenty-five to forty guests but operators limit numbers below maximum so deck space stays comfortable, you should not feel packed on a well-run cruise. Private charter catamarans for day hire are typically thirty-eight to fifty-five feet and take eight to fourteen guests cruising with room to spread out, dine and swim safely.

sailing.question: What is the best month for sailing in Rhodes?
sailing.answer: May, June, September and early October are the best months for sailing around Rhodes if you want warm sea, lighter winds and quieter anchorages without peak-summer crowds. July and August bring the strong Meltemi north winds, often Force four to seven, which experienced sailors enjoy for brisk passages but which can tire relaxed cruisers and families with young children.

sailing.question: What is the Meltemi wind and does it affect my trip?
sailing.answer: The Meltemi is a dry north-westerly wind that blows across the Aegean from late June through August, often reaching Force four to seven on exposed waters. On Rhodes east coast, where many day trips run, the island provides shelter and trips remain pleasant in many bays while open crossings toward Symi or Halki feel rougher.

sailing.question: Do I need any sailing experience?
sailing.answer: No, all our sailing trips include a professional skipper who handles the boat, reads weather and chooses anchorages. You are welcome to help with steering or raising sails if you wish, but you can also relax entirely with no prior experience. These are guest-friendly days, not sailing school unless you request learning time. Life jackets and a safety briefing are included on every departure.

sailing.question: Where can you sail to from Rhodes?
sailing.answer: Day sailing trips often explore the east coast toward Lindos, Kalithea, Anthony Quinn Bay and Faliraki with swim stops and lunch ashore or onboard. Half-day sails may stay closer to Mandraki bays and Kalithea Springs when time or wind limits range. Multi-day itineraries from Rhodes reach Symi in roughly ninety minutes in fair conditions, plus Tilos, Halki and Nisyros on longer legs of three to four hours.

sailing.question: Where do sailing trips depart from?
sailing.answer: Most sailing day trips depart from Mandraki Harbour in Rhodes Town, below the medieval walls and within walking distance of the Old Town, the same hub used by many cruises and private boats. Some east-coast day sails leave from Faliraki or Kolymbia to shorten transfer time for guests staying there.

sailing.question: What should I bring on a sailing trip?
sailing.answer: Pack light and practical: soft-soled shoes without black soles that mark decks, high-SPF sunscreen, a hat, sunglasses, a light windbreaker or long sleeve because it feels cooler on the water than on the beach, and swimwear under comfortable clothes. We provide towels, snorkel gear, water and snacks on most day trips, confirm in your quote.

cruises.question: What is included in a Rhodes day cruise?
cruises.answer: Most all-inclusive Rhodes day cruises include buffet lunch, Mediterranean dishes such as grilled chicken, Greek salad, pasta, bread and seasonal sides, plus unlimited soft drinks and often wine and beer, snorkel masks, and three to four swimming stops along the east coast or on routes toward nearby islands. Music, sun deck space and shaded seating are standard on larger vessels.

cruises.question: Where do Rhodes cruises depart from?
cruises.answer: Most day cruises depart from Mandraki Harbour in Rhodes Town, the historic harbour facing the deer statues and the Old Town walls. Some east-coast cruises leave from Faliraki, Kolymbia or Lindos when the route starts closer to those resorts, saving transfer time for guests staying there. Symi day cruises consistently leave Mandraki around 09:00 because the crossing is planned from town.

cruises.question: Is a day cruise different from a private boat trip?
cruises.answer: Yes, they target different priorities. A day cruise is a shared experience on a larger boat, often forty to eighty guests, with a fixed route, all-inclusive buffet and a set return time. It delivers the best value per person and a festive, social atmosphere ideal for first-time visitors who want everything organised without hiring a whole vessel.

cruises.question: Should I pick a catamaran day cruise or a monohull cruise in Rhodes?
cruises.answer: When booking a shared day cruise from Rhodes, hull type changes comfort more than the brochure photos suggest. Catamaran day cruises sit flat with minimal roll, offer more shade and deck space, and let you enter the water from a stable stern, most guests who compare both on the same route prefer the cat for swimming-and-lunch days.

cruises.question: Can you visit Symi in one day from Rhodes?
cruises.answer: Yes—you can visit Symi in one day from Rhodes without staying overnight. Shared day cruises and excursion boats leave Mandraki Harbour around 09:00, cross in roughly 75–90 minutes when the sea is calm, and give you four to five hours ashore in Gialos harbour before the return sailing, typically arriving back in Rhodes by 18:00.

cruises.question: Are sunset cruises worth it?
cruises.answer: Sunset cruises are among the most-booked evening activities in Rhodes because they combine a short sea outing with dinner, drinks and the skyline of the medieval city at golden hour, couples especially value the atmosphere.

thingsToDo.question: What are the best things to do in Rhodes by boat?
thingsToDo.answer: The biggest hits by boat from Rhodes are the Symi day cruise, swimming and snorkelling at Anthony Quinn Bay and Kalithea Springs, the Lindos seaward approach with views of the Acropolis, and a sunset catamaran cruise along the coast. For a more independent day, a license-free boat lets you explore east-coast bays at your own pace within the coastal zone.

thingsToDo.question: Should I book a boat tour or a day cruise?
thingsToDo.answer: Book a boat tour, private or semi-private on a smaller vessel with a skipper, when you want flexibility, a quieter deck and control over stops; ideal for couples, small families and groups celebrating something special.

thingsToDo.question: Which Rhodes boat experience is best for families?
thingsToDo.answer: Catamaran day cruises are usually the most family-friendly option: stable twin hulls in light chop, generous deck space, shaded seating, lunch included on board, and several swim stops in calm bays without rushing between tavernas. Children of any age are welcome; child-size life jackets are provided and crew are accustomed to families. Shared large cruises also work for families who want simplicity and inclusivity at lower per-head cost.

thingsToDo.question: What is the best boat trip from Rhodes?
thingsToDo.answer: The best boat trip from Rhodes depends on what you want from the day, not a single brochure winner. The Symi day cruise is the most booked route: boats leave Mandraki around 09:00, you spend four to five hours in the pastel harbour of Gialos, and return to Rhodes by about 18:00—a full island day without overnight luggage.

thingsToDo.question: How should I plan multiple boat days during one Rhodes holiday?
thingsToDo.answer: Most visitors with a week on Rhodes enjoy two or three distinct days on the water rather than repeating the same east-coast swim route. A sensible pattern is one full east-coast day cruise or private trip early in the holiday, one signature outing such as Symi or Lindos mid-week, and a sunset catamaran or license-free rental when you want a lighter half-day.

thingsToDo.question: Can I see Symi or Lindos by boat from Rhodes?
thingsToDo.answer: Yes, both are classic full-day boat experiences from Rhodes and appear in our catalog as shared cruises and private trips. Symi is roughly an eighty-minute crossing each way; you spend most of the day on the island in the famous harbour.

serviceHub.question: Which service should I pick, rent a boat, boat tour or yacht charter?
serviceHub.answer: The right service depends on how much independence you want and how many days you have on Rhodes. A license-free rental suits couples and small families who want to swim in their own bay at their own pace along the sheltered east coast, you drive after a short briefing.

serviceHub.question: What does each service typically cost?
serviceHub.answer: License-free rentals start at the lower end and depend on boat size and hours, typically a day rate for coastal use with fuel for the agreed zone. Shared boat tours and day cruises are priced per person, often €40–€80 for a full east-coast day with lunch included.

serviceHub.question: Are these services run by you directly or by partners?
serviceHub.answer: A mix of both, by design. Some boats and yachts are operated directly by us or our closest associates; others are run by trusted local captains and small family-owned operators we have worked with for years along the Rhodes coast and in the Dodecanese.

serviceHub.question: Can I combine multiple services on the same trip?
serviceHub.answer: Yes, most visitors combine two or three services during one holiday, and we help you sequence them sensibly. A common pairing is a license-free rental day plus a Symi shared cruise the next day, or a sunset catamaran paired with a sidecar tour on land. Another pattern is private boat trip early in the week and a relaxed east-coast cruise later when you want an all-inclusive buffet without planning.

serviceHub.question: Do you offer transfers from my hotel to the harbour?
serviceHub.answer: Yes. We provide private transfers from hotels, villas and the airport across Rhodes to Mandraki Harbour, Faliraki, Kolymbia, Lindos and other departure points used by our boat services. Meet-and-greet, child seats on request and timing aligned with cruise check-in are standard requests we handle regularly, especially for Symi days that leave around 09:00.

serviceHub.question: Which services are available year-round?
serviceHub.answer: Yacht charters and private skippered boat trips generally run from late April through late October, with exact fleet dates varying by operator maintenance schedules. Daily shared cruises and organised boat tours operate from May to mid-October, with peak availability June through September when every hull is active. License-free rentals follow similar seasonal windows because small open boats are weather-dependent in winter.
